guideline: Limit primary navigation to 5-7 items
rationale: Miller's Law: working memory holds 7±2 items. More items cause decision paralysis.

guideline: Use breadcrumbs for hierarchies deeper than 2 levels
rationale: Users need wayfinding in deep hierarchies to avoid feeling lost.

guideline: Place primary CTA in top-right of navigation bar
rationale: Eye-tracking shows users scan top-right after logo. CTA there captures maximum attention.

guideline: Make mobile hamburger menu accessible via ARIA
rationale: Mobile nav menus must be keyboard and screen-reader accessible.

guideline: Use sticky navigation on long-scroll pages
rationale: Persistent nav reduces scroll-to-top friction and improves task completion.

guideline: Validate on blur first then on change for subsequent edits
rationale: Immediate validation is annoying. Blur-then-change balances feedback timing.

guideline: Place labels above inputs not inline or beside
rationale: Top-aligned labels have fastest completion time in eye-tracking studies.

guideline: Use a single-column layout for forms
rationale: Single column forms are 15% faster to complete than multi-column.

guideline: Show password strength meter for signup
rationale: Users create stronger passwords when given real-time feedback.

guideline: Disable submit button during processing show spinner
rationale: Prevents double-submission and gives users confidence action is processing.

guideline: Scroll to first error on submit with smooth animation
rationale: Users need immediate awareness of what went wrong and where.

guideline: Group related fields with section headers
rationale: Chunking reduces cognitive load and makes long forms feel manageable.

guideline: Pre-fill known data to reduce entry burden
rationale: WCAG 3.3.7 Redundant Entry: don't ask for info you already have.

guideline: Make primary CTAs visually dominant over secondary
rationale: Visual hierarchy guides users to the most important action.

guideline: Minimum touch target 44x44px on mobile
rationale: WCAG 2.5.8: touch targets below 44px cause tap errors on mobile.

guideline: Use verbs for button labels not nouns
rationale: 'Save Changes' is clearer than 'Changes'. Action-oriented labels reduce ambiguity.

guideline: Place primary action on the right secondary on left
rationale: Western left-to-right reading pattern makes right position feel like forward progress.

guideline: Limit to 2 visible actions per context
rationale: Too many buttons cause decision paralysis. Move extras to dropdown menus.

guideline: Show toast notifications for non-critical success
rationale: Toasts are non-blocking and appropriate for confirmations like 'Saved'.

guideline: Use inline alerts for errors requiring user action
rationale: Errors need persistent visible placement near the problem source.

guideline: Auto-dismiss success toasts after 5 seconds
rationale: Success messages don't need persistent visibility. 5s gives time to read.

guideline: Pause toast auto-dismiss on hover
rationale: Users reading a toast shouldn't lose it mid-sentence.

guideline: Limit concurrent toasts to 3 maximum
rationale: Toast stacking beyond 3 creates visual noise and confusion.

guideline: Use skeleton screens over spinners for content loading
rationale: Skeletons feel faster than spinners by setting layout expectations.

guideline: Show skeleton shapes that match final content layout
rationale: Matching skeletons to content layout prevents jarring layout shifts.

guideline: Use progress bars for operations over 4 seconds
rationale: Long operations need progress indication to prevent user abandonment.

guideline: Add shimmer animation to skeletons for perceived speed
rationale: Shimmer animation signals active loading and feels faster than static gray.

guideline: Disable buttons and show spinners during form submission
rationale: Prevents double-submission and gives clear processing feedback.

guideline: Never convey information by color alone
rationale: WCAG 1.4.1: colorblind users miss color-only status indicators.

guideline: Provide text alternatives for all non-text content
rationale: WCAG 1.1.1: screen readers need text descriptions for images and icons.

guideline: Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard accessible
rationale: WCAG 2.1.1: keyboard-only users must reach and operate all controls.

guideline: Use ARIA labels for icon-only buttons
rationale: Icon-only buttons are meaningless to screen readers without labels.

guideline: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query
rationale: Vestibular disorders cause nausea from motion. Always provide reduced-motion.

guideline: Maintain visible focus indicators on all focusable elements
rationale: WCAG 2.4.7: keyboard users need to see where focus is.

guideline: Use semantic HTML elements for page structure
rationale: Semantic HTML provides free accessibility: nav main section header footer.

guideline: Implement skip-to-content link as first focusable element
rationale: Keyboard users should not tab through entire nav to reach content.

guideline: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text
rationale: WCAG 1.4.3: minimum contrast for readable text on all backgrounds.

guideline: Ensure 3:1 contrast ratio for UI components
rationale: WCAG 1.4.11: inputs borders and controls need adequate contrast.

guideline: Use 4px base grid for spacing consistency
rationale: 4px grid ensures mathematical harmony and prevents arbitrary spacing.

guideline: Limit content width to 1280px for readability
rationale: Lines exceeding 75-80 characters per line reduce reading speed.

guideline: Use 8-column grid on tablet and 4-column on mobile
rationale: Column reduction maintains readability as viewport narrows.

guideline: Add container padding that scales with viewport
rationale: Clamp-based padding prevents content touching edges on any screen size.

guideline: Stack horizontal layouts vertically on mobile
rationale: Horizontal layouts must gracefully collapse for narrow viewports.

guideline: Use 16px minimum for body text
rationale: Sub-16px body text causes readability issues especially on mobile.

guideline: Limit to 2-3 font families maximum
rationale: More than 3 fonts creates visual chaos and hurts performance.

guideline: Use fluid type scale with clamp() for responsive sizing
rationale: Fluid type eliminates jarring size jumps between breakpoints.

guideline: Maintain 1.5-1.6 line height for body text
rationale: Adequate line height improves readability especially for long paragraphs.

guideline: Use -0.01 to -0.02em letter-spacing for large headings
rationale: Large text needs negative tracking to appear optically balanced.

guideline: Use semantic color tokens not raw hex values
rationale: Semantic tokens enable theme switching and maintain consistency.

guideline: Define light and dark mode for every palette
rationale: Users expect dark mode support. Both modes must pass WCAG.

guideline: Use brand-muted color for selected or active state backgrounds
rationale: Muted brand color signals active state without overwhelming.

guideline: Reserve red for errors and destructive actions
rationale: Red has strong error association. Using it decoratively causes confusion.

guideline: Use consistent semantic colors across all palettes
rationale: Success=green Warning=amber Error=red Info=blue across all themes.

guideline: Enter slower and exit faster
rationale: Users need time to perceive entering elements. Exits can be quick.

guideline: Nearby elements animate faster than distant ones
rationale: Hover states 150ms. Modals from off-screen 400ms. Match physical distance.

guideline: Use ease-out for entering elements and ease-in for exiting
rationale: Ease-out decelerates into view naturally. Ease-in accelerates out.

guideline: Limit stagger delay to 5-6 items maximum
rationale: Staggering more than 6 items makes the sequence feel sluggish.

guideline: Replace all animation with instant opacity for reduced-motion
rationale: Reduced motion users should still see content appear via opacity.

guideline: Follow the proven homepage section order
rationale: Nav>Hero>Social-Proof>Features>Showcase>How-It-Works>Testimonials>Pricing>CTA>Footer

guideline: Use card-based layouts for browseable content
rationale: Cards provide consistent scannable containers for repeated content.

guideline: Implement empty states for all data-dependent views
rationale: Empty states guide users to take action rather than showing blank screens.

guideline: Place destructive settings in a red zone at page bottom
rationale: Physical separation and color coding prevent accidental destructive actions.

guideline: Use side navigation for apps with 5+ pages
rationale: Side nav scales better than top nav for deep page hierarchies.

guideline: Use data tables for structured multi-column data
rationale: Tables are the most efficient format for comparing structured data.

guideline: Provide sort and filter controls above data tables
rationale: Users need to find specific data in large datasets quickly.

guideline: Use pagination for datasets over 50 rows
rationale: Unpaginated large datasets cause performance and usability issues.

guideline: Show loading skeletons that match table layout
rationale: Table skeletons set expectations for data structure and prevent layout shift.

guideline: Highlight selected rows with brand-muted background
rationale: Subtle highlight makes selected items visually distinct without overwhelming.

guideline: Use bottom navigation for mobile apps with 3-5 top-level sections
rationale: Bottom nav is within thumb reach and follows mobile platform conventions.

guideline: Make all interactive elements minimum 44px tap target
rationale: WCAG touch target requirements prevent frustrating mis-taps.

guideline: Use sheet modals sliding from bottom on mobile
rationale: Bottom sheets are more thumb-friendly than centered modals on mobile.

guideline: Collapse complex filters into a filter sheet on mobile
rationale: Filter panels don't fit mobile layouts — use expandable sheets.

guideline: Test touch interactions with real fingers not just cursor
rationale: Mouse precision differs from finger precision. Always test on devices.

guideline: Lazy-load images below the fold
rationale: Deferred image loading improves initial page load time.

guideline: Use system fonts or limit to 2 Google Fonts
rationale: Each font family adds 20-100KB. Minimize for performance.

guideline: Inline critical CSS above the fold
rationale: Critical CSS inlining prevents render-blocking stylesheet requests.

guideline: Aim for sub-2.5s Largest Contentful Paint
rationale: Core Web Vital threshold for good user experience.

guideline: Compress and serve images in WebP or AVIF format
rationale: Modern formats reduce image sizes by 25-50% versus JPEG/PNG.

guideline: Override shadows with glow-border technique in dark mode
rationale: Standard shadows are invisible on dark backgrounds. Glow-borders provide visible elevation.

guideline: Use slightly elevated background colors for depth not shadow
rationale: Dark mode depth comes from surface color variation not shadow opacity.

guideline: Reduce image brightness and saturation in dark mode to prevent glare
rationale: Full-brightness images on dark backgrounds create harsh contrast and eye strain.

guideline: Provide a system-preference-aware toggle defaulting to OS preference
rationale: Users expect dark mode to follow their OS setting via prefers-color-scheme media query.

guideline: Use skeleton screens shaped to match the actual content layout
rationale: Content-shaped skeletons set accurate expectations and reduce perceived load time by 30%.

guideline: Show progressive loading for heavy pages — header first then content
rationale: Progressive rendering gives users something to see immediately while the rest loads.

guideline: Show inline validation errors immediately below the invalid field
rationale: Errors placed near the source are found 3x faster than summary-only error messages.

guideline: Use constructive error messages that suggest how to fix the problem
rationale: 'Password must be 8+ characters' is actionable. 'Invalid input' is not.

guideline: Preserve user input when displaying errors — never clear the form
rationale: Clearing form data on error forces users to re-enter everything causing abandonment.

guideline: Include a clear call-to-action in empty states not just a message
rationale: Empty states are onboarding opportunities. Guide users to their first action.

guideline: Use illustrations or icons in empty states to reduce visual emptiness
rationale: Visual elements in empty states feel intentional rather than broken or unfinished.

guideline: Add subtle hover state transitions at 150ms for interactive elements
rationale: 150ms transitions feel responsive. Longer feels sluggish. Shorter feels jarring.

guideline: Animate button press with a slight scale-down for tactile feedback
rationale: Scale(0.97) on active gives physical press sensation on screen elements.

guideline: Hide advanced options behind an expandable section or toggle
rationale: Progressive disclosure reduces initial complexity while keeping power-user features accessible.

guideline: Show only 3-5 options initially then reveal more with a show-more control
rationale: Fewer initial options speed decision-making. Hick's Law: time increases with choices.

guideline: Space touch targets at least 8px apart to prevent accidental taps
rationale: Adjacent touch targets need padding to prevent mis-taps on touchscreens.

guideline: Place destructive actions away from frequently used controls on mobile
rationale: Physical separation prevents accidental destructive taps during routine mobile interactions.

guideline: Support swipe-to-delete with a visible undo option for 5 seconds
rationale: Swipe gestures need undo safety nets since they're easy to trigger accidentally.

guideline: Provide visual feedback during voice input to confirm the system is listening
rationale: Users need confirmation that voice input is active to avoid talking to nothing.

guideline: Show confidence levels or source attribution for AI-generated content
rationale: Users need transparency about AI output reliability to make informed decisions.

guideline: Provide easy editing controls for AI-generated suggestions
rationale: AI output should be a starting point not a final answer — make editing frictionless.

guideline: Pin the first column and header row in scrollable data tables
rationale: Pinned headers and ID columns maintain context while scrolling large datasets.

guideline: Provide column resize handles and a density toggle for data tables
rationale: Users have different density preferences. Let them adjust row height and column widths.

guideline: Offer compact normal and comfortable density modes for dashboards
rationale: Power users prefer dense views. New users prefer comfortable spacing.

guideline: Group related notifications and allow batch dismiss
rationale: Notification grouping prevents inbox overload and reduces dismissal fatigue.

guideline: Use different notification urgency levels with distinct visual treatments
rationale: Critical alerts need red and persistent display. Info can be subtle and auto-dismiss.

guideline: Limit onboarding to 3-5 steps with a skip option and progress indicator
rationale: Long onboarding causes 60% drop-off. Short focused flows with skip improve completion.

guideline: Use contextual tooltips on first use instead of upfront tutorials
rationale: In-context guidance at point-of-use is retained 5x better than upfront tutorials.

guideline: Show search suggestions after 2+ characters with debounced 300ms delay
rationale: Instant suggestions improve findability. Debouncing prevents excessive API calls.

guideline: Display recent searches and popular queries in empty search state
rationale: Recent and popular suggestions reduce typing effort and inspire discovery.

guideline: Use infinite scroll for browsing feeds and pagination for search results
rationale: Feeds benefit from continuous scrolling. Search results need page-based navigation for findability.

guideline: Serve responsive images with srcset and appropriate sizes attribute
rationale: Different viewport sizes need different image resolutions to save bandwidth and load time.

guideline: Validate email format on blur and check availability on submit
rationale: Format validation on blur gives immediate feedback without premature availability checks.

guideline: Show password requirements as a checklist that updates in real-time
rationale: Real-time requirement checklists are 40% more effective than post-submit error messages.

guideline: Place cookie consent in a non-blocking bottom banner with clear reject option
rationale: Non-blocking banners respect user flow. Easy reject respects privacy regulations and user trust.

guideline: Display keyboard shortcut hints in tooltips and provide a shortcut reference modal
rationale: Keyboard shortcuts increase power-user efficiency 40% when discoverable through tooltips.
